chapters.title: Module 1 - Chord Melody Quickstart
chapters.notes: Bird's-eye 4-step overview of arranging chord melodies: picking a tune, changing key for range/playability, learning the melody on top 2-3 strings, choosing chord voicings below melody notes. Also covers right-hand techniques for chord melody. Front matter (pp 1-14: TOC, intro, course overview, listening exercises) is skipped per schema rules.

chapters.title: Module 2 - Chord Vocabulary
chapters.notes: Three Harmonic Families (Joe Pass concept), building 'Your First Chord Collection' on G major via the 5-step Arpeggio -> Harmonize -> Add Scale Notes -> Harmonize Scale -> Transpose process; A Summer's Day exercise; Major ii-V-I and minor ii-V-i chord collections (incl. 2nd-string variants); Understanding Chord Labels (construction, extensions, altered tensions, slash chords, same-notes-different-names, finding voicings).

chapters.title: Module 3 - Let's Create a Chord Melody Arrangement (First Steps & Harmony Analysis)
chapters.notes: First half of Module 3. Chord Melody Arrangement First Steps: finding a suitable tune, listening to recordings, pencil-and-paper vs software, choosing a key, learning the melody on top 2-3 strings. Then Learn the Chords, Analyze the Harmony, Analyze Intervals in the Harmony.

chapters.title: Module 3 - Painting by Numbers and Arrangement Examples
chapters.notes: Second half of Module 3. Painting by Numbers - Adding Chords to the Melody: applying chords to the melody line, choosing voicings, making arrangements playable, adding tension to dominant chords. Worked examples: Fly Me to the Moon and Alone Together basic chord melody arrangements.

chapters.title: Module 4 - Arranging For Solo Guitar (Techniques)
chapters.notes: First half of Module 4. Introduction to arranging for solo guitar, bass lines in solo guitar, key selection and open strings, useful chord voicings (shell voicings + adding melody notes), Moon River step-by-step solo arrangement walkthrough, arpeggiation, octaves and diads, walking bass techniques, passing chords.

chapters.title: Module 4 - Solo Guitar Arrangement Examples
chapters.notes: Second half of Module 4. Worked solo guitar arrangements: Example 1 - My Funny Valentine, Example 2 - Don't Get Around Much Anymore.

chapters.title: Module 5 - More Advanced Arranging Techniques
chapters.notes: Module 5 intro, then five substitution approaches: (1) Tritone Substitution (explained + examples + harmonizing a melody with it); (2) Major-to-Minor (relative substitution, I-to-iii, examples); (3) Minor 7th for Dominant 7th (explanation + examples); (4) Back Cycling; (5) Melodic Common Tone Substitution. Then adding jazz licks/phrases, completing your arrangement and getting ready to perform, complete performance example of Fly Me to The Moon, wrap-up.

chapters.title: Appendix 1 - Chord Dictionaries
chapters.notes: Reference appendix collecting voicings used in the course: Chord Melody Dictionary (featured in Module 3) and Solo Guitar Chord Dictionary (featured in Module 4).

chapters.title: Appendix 2 - Fingerstyle Training
chapters.notes: Fingerstyle technique appendix: introduction to fingerstyle guitar, finger walking and string crossing (walking finger workouts, fingerstyle string crossing), using the thumb, two/three/four-string exercises, fingerstyle etudes (Etude 1 - Walking Bass Bb Blues; Etude 2 - Bossa Nova Comping Etude). Excludes the single-page About The Author at p 318.
